---
title: "Presentation Title"
subtitle: "Subtitle"
author: "Author Name"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: dark
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    preview-links: auto
    logo: ""
    footer: "Footer text"
    incremental: false
    code-line-numbers: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    highlight-style: github
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false

library(tidyverse)
library(gt)
library(plotly)

# Set global theme
theme_set(theme_minimal(base_size = 16))

# Load data
data <- mtcars %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "model") %>%
  as_tibble()
```

# Introduction {background-color="#2C3E50"}

## Overview

::: {.incremental}
- **Point 1**: First key point
- **Point 2**: Second key point
- **Point 3**: Third key point
:::

::: {.notes}
Speaker notes go here - not visible to audience.
Remember to emphasize the key findings.
:::

## Objectives

::: {.callout-note}
## Research Questions

1. What is the relationship between variables?
2. How do different groups compare?
3. What patterns emerge from the data?
:::

# Background {background-color="#34495E"}

## Context

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Left Column**

- Point one
- Point two
- Point three
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Right Column**

- Additional context
- Supporting information
- Key details
:::

::::

## Literature Review

> "Relevant quote or finding from literature"
>
> --- Author Name

::: {.fragment}
This finding is important because...
:::

# Methodology {background-color="#2C3E50"}

## Data Collection

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| code-line-numbers: "|1|2-3|4-5"

# Load and prepare data
data_clean <- data %>%
  select(model, mpg, hp, wt, cyl) %>%
  filter(!is.na(mpg)) %>%
  mutate(efficiency = mpg / wt)
```

## Sample Description

```{r}
#| label: tbl-sample
#| tbl-cap: "Sample characteristics"

data %>%
  summarise(
    N = n(),
    `Mean MPG` = mean(mpg),
    `SD MPG` = sd(mpg),
    `Mean HP` = mean(hp),
    `SD HP` = sd(hp)
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  fmt_number(columns = everything(), decimals = 1) %>%
  tab_header(title = "Sample Statistics")
```

## Analysis Approach

::: {.r-stack}
![](image1.png){.fragment width="450"}

![](image2.png){.fragment width="450"}

![](image3.png){.fragment width="450"}
:::

# Results {background-color="#34495E"}

## Key Finding 1

```{r}
#| label: fig-result1
#| fig-cap: "Distribution of fuel efficiency"
#| output-location: column

ggplot(data, aes(x = mpg)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2,
                 fill = "#3498DB",
                 alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(
    x = "Miles Per Gallon",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```

::: {.notes}
Note the distribution pattern - most vehicles cluster around 15-20 MPG.
:::

## Key Finding 2 {.smaller}

```{r}
#| label: fig-result2
#| fig-cap: "Relationship between weight and efficiency"
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5

p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = wt, y = mpg, color = as.factor(cyl))) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Cylinders") +
  labs(
    x = "Weight (1000 lbs)",
    y = "Miles Per Gallon"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

ggplotly(p)
```

## Key Finding 3

::: {layout-ncol=2}
```{r}
#| fig-cap: "By cylinder count"

ggplot(data, aes(x = as.factor(cyl), y = mpg, fill = as.factor(cyl))) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(x = "Cylinders", y = "MPG") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```

```{r}
#| fig-cap: "By horsepower"

ggplot(data, aes(x = hp, y = mpg)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6, color = "#E74C3C") +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "#2C3E50") +
  labs(x = "Horsepower", y = "MPG") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```
:::

## Statistical Results

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| output-location: fragment

# Fit regression model
model <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl, data = data)

# Display summary
broom::tidy(model) %>%
  select(term, estimate, p.value) %>%
  gt() %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(estimate, p.value), decimals = 3)
```

# Discussion {background-color="#2C3E50"}

## Interpretation

::: {.callout-important}
## Key Takeaway

The analysis reveals a strong negative relationship between vehicle weight and fuel efficiency, with engine configuration playing a significant role.
:::

## Comparison with Literature

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="40%"}
**Our Findings**

- Finding A
- Finding B
- Finding C
:::

::: {.column width="60%"}
**Literature**

- Similar result from Study X
- Contrasting finding from Study Y
- New contribution to field
:::

::::

## Limitations

::: {.callout-warning}
## Considerations

1. **Sample size**: Limited to `r nrow(data)` vehicles
2. **Data age**: Historical dataset from 1974
3. **Scope**: Only covers specific vehicle types
:::

# Conclusion {background-color="#34495E"}

## Summary

::: {.r-fit-text}
Main findings in brief
:::

::: {.incremental}
1. **Finding 1**: Weight strongly predicts fuel efficiency
2. **Finding 2**: Cylinder count affects performance
3. **Finding 3**: Modern implications for vehicle design
:::

## Implications

::: {.callout-tip}
## Practical Applications

- Application in industry
- Policy recommendations
- Future research directions
:::

## Future Work

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
**Next Steps**

1. Expand sample to modern vehicles
2. Include electric vehicles in analysis
3. Examine regional differences
:::

# Thank You! {background-color="#2C3E50"}

::: {.r-fit-text}
Questions?
:::

**Contact Information**

- Email: author@example.com
- Website: https://example.com
- GitHub: @username

## References {.smaller}

::: {#refs}
:::

---

## Appendix: Additional Analysis

```{r}
#| label: fig-appendix
#| fig-cap: "Supplementary visualization"

ggplot(data, aes(x = qsec, y = mpg, color = as.factor(cyl))) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Cylinders") +
  labs(
    x = "Quarter Mile Time (seconds)",
    y = "Miles Per Gallon"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```
